analysis: Track A: ORC Phase Transition in Brain Connectomes

findings.hypothesis_strict_reason: No zero-crossing of κ̄ detected at any η for any subject. Brain connectomes show κ̄ > 0 (spherical geometry) at all tested densities, unlike semantic networks which have κ̄ < 0 at η < 2.5.
findings.hypothesis_weak_reason: TDC (healthy) subjects reach lower minimum κ̄ (0.1536) than ADHD (0.2328), Δ = 0.0792. Healthy brain networks are geometrically CLOSER to Euclidean (κ̄ → 0) than ADHD networks at matched density thresholds. This is consistent with the spirit of the sweet-spot hypothesis: healthy networks operate near the Euclidean critical geometry.
findings.clinical_finding: ADHD brains show HIGHER ORC (Δ = +0.0792) than TDC at minimum-κ̄ density. This is consistent with hypersynchrony / over-connectivity in ADHD: more clique-like (spherical) topology, less tree-like (hyperbolic).
methodological_notes: MSDL atlas (39 ROIs) — coarser than typical parcellations
No global signal regression (global signal inflates positive FC)
Binary adjacency (no edge weights) — consistent with Lean formalization
next_steps: Apply global signal regression to reveal negative-ORC regime
Collect more subjects (full ADHD-200 dataset, N=40)
Test on HCP Lifespan dataset for broader comparison
